title: JEE Physics Competitive Plugin
description: Targeted competitive preparation modules for JEE Main & Advanced — Physics edge content across all Class 11 & 12 chapters
copyrightDisclaimer: © Textbook content belongs exclusively to its respective authors and authorities. IIS Academy content offers enhancement, supplemental insights, and technology-powered add-ons. Use strictly for educational purposes.

modules.subject: Physics
patternRecognition: JEE loves: Electric field due to continuous charge distributions — always set up the integral
JEE loves: System of charges energy — count ALL pairs, use U = kq₁q₂/r
JEE loves: Potential and field in conducting/cavity problems
NEET loves: Field line diagrams, Faraday cage, dielectric intuition
calculationHacks: E due to uniformly charged ring at axis: E = kQx/(x²+R²)^(3/2); peaks at x = R/√2
E due to infinite line charge: E = λ/(2πε₀r) — use λ = charge per unit length
E due to infinite plane: E = σ/(2ε₀) — independent of distance!
For two concentric shells, always use superposition

highDifficultyProblems.description: Find E on axis of uniformly charged disc
highDifficultyProblems.solution: E_disc = σ/(2ε₀) × [1 - x/√(x²+R²)] — derived by integrating ring formula over radius

highDifficultyProblems.description: Three charges at corners of equilateral triangle — find total PE
highDifficultyProblems.solution: U = k[q₁q₂/a + q₂q₃/a + q₁q₃/a] — sum all 3 pairs × Coulomb's constant

mnemonicAgents: CAGE = Coulomb → Applications → Gauss → Energy density
'Shells shield' — conductor shields interior from external E field
PLANE stays SAME — E from infinite plane is constant (doesn't depend on distance)

modules.subject: Physics
patternRecognition: JEE loves: Constraint motion — Atwood machine variants with pulleys
JEE loves: Pseudo force in non-inertial frames (accelerating cars, elevators)
JEE loves: Friction problems with angled surfaces and multiple blocks
NEET loves: Conceptual questions on inertia and Newton's laws
calculationHacks: Apparent weight in lift: N = m(g±a) — add when accelerating up, subtract when down
Spring scale reads normal force, NOT actual weight
In inclined plane: resolve along & perpendicular to incline, not horizontal/vertical
mnemonicAgents: 'Friction first finds static limit, then kinetic kicks in'
LIFT: Load direction determines apparent weight: Up→N increases, Down→N decreases

modules.subject: Physics
patternRecognition: JEE loves: Force on current-carrying conductor in magnetic field
JEE loves: Motion of charged particle in combined E and B fields
JEE loves: Biot-Savart Law for circular loop, solenoid, toroid
calculationHacks: Radius of circular motion in B field: r = mv/(qB) = p/(qB)
Time period independent of v: T = 2πm/(qB) — cyclotron frequency
Force on straight wire: F = BIL sin θ
B at centre of circular loop: B = μ₀I/(2R)
mnemonicAgents: FIBS = Force Is Biot-Savart (for field) and F=qvB (for force on charge)
Right-hand rule: thumb points current, fingers curl → field direction
